title: jatropha cuneata
intro: Jatropha cuneata is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the 'cork bush' or 'Mexican corkscrew'. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and stems of Jatropha cuneata have been used to make herbal preparations, including infusions and salves, which can also provide health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving pain. Additionally, the plant's sap has been used to treat skin conditions and wounds, and its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-cancer and antioxidant properties.
